metadata.notes:
agent_supports: no
daemon_capability: n/a; driver_capability: n/a

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: no
daemon_capability: n/a; driver_capability: n/a

## Primitive exercised

Background process management (`run_in_background: true` or shell `&`) — the agent can launch a detached shell process and the daemon tracks its liveness *separately from* transcript turn boundaries: `ready → working` at the prompt, `BackgroundProcessCount > 0` while the process is alive, the session stays `working` past the turn's own end, then a single `working → ready` once the process exits.

## Reasoning — agent_supports: no

pi (v0.70.0) has no fire-and-forget background-process primitive. Three independent confirmations:

1. **Tool schema.** pi's built-in `bash` tool (`dist/core/tools/bash.js`) declares exactly two parameters — `command: string` and `timeout?: number`. There is no `run_in_background` / `background` field the model can set. The only knob is a foreground timeout.
2. **Synchronous execution.** `bash.execute()` returns a Promise that resolves only after `waitForChildProcess(child)` reports the child has terminated (it awaits `ops.exec(...)` to completion). The tool result is emitted at process exit, so a bash call always finishes *inside* the turn — there is no detached handle returned to the model to poll later.
3. **`detached: true` is a red herring.** The Step 2.5 strings/source scan surfaced `detached: true` in `createLocalBashOperations` — but it is paired with `trackDetachedChildPid` / `killProcessTree` and exists purely to put the shell in its own process group so pi can SIGKILL the whole tree on timeout or ESC-abort. It is process-group hygiene, not backgrounding; the parent still blocks on exit.

The pi README explicitly disclaims the feature: **"No background bash. Use tmux. Full observability, direct interaction."** (README.md:460, alongside the matching "No sub-agents / No plan mode / No MCP" not-built list). Stock pi therefore cannot produce the `working`-past-turn-end arc the scenario requires; a user who wants a detached process is told to run it under tmux out-of-band, where it is a separate OS process pi never tracks.

## Reasoning — daemon_capability: n/a; driver_capability: n/a

Because the agent lacks the feature, there is nothing to observe and no recording is producible — hence `n/a` rather than `no`. Independently, the transport could not observe it even if pi had it: the pi parser (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/parser.go`) handles only `session`, `model_change`, `thinking_level_change`/`branch_summary`/`custom`, `compaction`, and `type:message` (roles assistant/user/toolResult/bashExecution). It has no `backgroundTaskId` extraction and emits no `BackgroundProcessCount` contribution — the entire #450/#445 mechanism (structured `backgroundTaskId` + `lsof` liveness probe gating `IsAgentDone`) is claudecode-specific and not wired for the pi `FilesUnderRoot` source.

## STRUCTURAL CONCERN — undefined requires-key (matrix applicability is definitionally undefined)

This scenario declares `requires: [tool_calls, background_process]` in `scenarios.json`. The capability id **`background_process` does NOT exist** in `replaydata/agents/features.json` (whose `features` map has no such key — it has `tool_calls`, `subagents`, `permission_hooks`, etc. but nothing for background processes), nor is there a `background_process` key in `replaydata/agents/pi/capabilities.json`. Until the maintainer either (a) adds the `background_process` capability id to `features.json` (and declares it per-adapter) or (b) corrects the `requires` array on the scenario, the matrix *applicability* of this cell is **definitionally undefined** — the requires-resolution can't be evaluated. The underlying-behavior verdict above stands regardless of how that key question is resolved: pi does not support background processes either way.

## Drift vs. prior matrix

Coverage row currently says `agent_supports: yes`, `daemon_capability: n/a; driver_capability: n/a` (notes empty). This assessment ratifies the column reassessment's flip: the prior `yes` overstated agent support (pi has no background-bash primitive at all). Corrected verdict is `no` / `n/a` (`applicable: no`). The maintainer should update the coverage row.
caveats: Undefined requires-key: scenario `requires: [tool_calls, background_process]` references capability id `background_process` which is absent from replaydata/agents/features.json and from pi's capabilities.json — matrix applicability for this cell is definitionally undefined until the maintainer adds the key or fixes the requires array. The behavior verdict (no) holds regardless.

`detached: true` appears in pi's bash tool source but is process-group hygiene for killProcessTree on timeout/abort, not a model-facing run_in_background feature — the bash tool still blocks on child exit and exposes no background parameter.
If a user runs a detached process via tmux (pi's documented workaround) it is a separate OS process pi never tracks in its transcript, so it is invisible to the pi adapter regardless.
Even hypothetically, the pi parser has no backgroundTaskId / BackgroundProcessCount path; the #450 lsof-probe mechanism is claudecode-specific.

sources.note: v0.70.0 bash tool: bashSchema has only {command, timeout}; execute() awaits waitForChildProcess(child) to completion (synchronous); detached:true is paired with trackDetachedChildPid/killProcessTree for process-group teardown, not backgrounding — no run_in_background param.
Line 460: 'No background bash. Use tmux. Full observability, direct interaction.' — explicit not-built disclaimer (v0.70.0).

ParseLine handles only session/model_change/thinking_level_change/branch_summary/custom/compaction/message(roles assistant,user,toolResult,bashExecution) — no backgroundTaskId extraction, no BackgroundProcessCount contribution.
Source: FilesUnderRoot + ExactName{pi} — observes a local transcript + local PID only; the background-process arc the spec asserts is the claudecode #450 mechanism, not wired here.

features map has no `background_process` key (only tool_calls/subagents/permission_hooks/etc.) — the scenario's requires-key is undefined; matrix applicability undefined until maintainer adds it or fixes requires.
No `background_process` key present for pi; cost_tracking=false, subagents=false, permission_hooks=false — confirms the requires-key is unmapped at the adapter level too.
pi design-rationale blog post (linked from README) framing pi as a local, fully-observable terminal agent with deliberately-omitted features.
